title: Help — Atrio FM Chatbot
subtitleBeforeRepo: Administrator and user guide. For the full Polish handbook, see

overview.intro: Atrio FM Chatbot helps tenants and staff with building questions and service requests. Admins manage tickets, users, knowledge documents, and quality improvements.

cards.title: Chat
cards.body: Ask questions; the bot may create a service ticket when it detects a real issue.

cards.title: Admin
cards.body: Tickets, knowledge gaps, users, documents, training data, and quality tools.

cards.title: Quality
cards.body: Group errors, run evaluations, apply safe prompt tweaks without code.

rows: Questions the bot could not answer from docs
Edit the base system prompt for chat

calloutSignIn.title: First-time admin sign-in
calloutSignIn.body: Open /admin/login (or use the main sign-in if your account is admin). Default username is often admin; password is set by your technical contact.

calloutDaily.title: Daily habit
calloutDaily.body: Check Knowledge gaps regularly. Each gap is a missing answer in your documentation.

steps: Type your question or issue at the bottom.
Read the reply, category, priority, and whether a ticket was created.
chat.whenTicketHeading: When the bot usually creates a ticket

cards.title: Creates a ticket
cards.body: Physical fault, safety risk, HVAC / electrical / plumbing / elevators, clear repair need.

cards.title: Often no ticket
cards.body: Hours, policies, “where is…”, thank-yous, status of an existing ticket (informational only).

rows: Immediate danger to people or property
Fire, gas smell, flooding electrical hazard
No heat in winter, server room cooling down
Stuck door, broken light in corridor

chat.createAnywayBody: If the bot answered but you still need a ticket, use the button under the message (manual ticket).
chat.newChatBody: Use New chat when you change topic. The bot remembers recent messages in the active thread.

calloutRateLimit.title: Rate limit
calloutRateLimit.body: Roughly 30 chat messages per minute per user. If you see “too many requests”, wait a minute.

calloutDoNot.title: Do not
list: Rely on chat alone for life-safety emergencies — call your emergency line.
Spam the same question; it confuses context.
Assume a ticket exists without checking the dashboard.

dashboard.intro: Admins see all tickets; regular users see only their own. Use filters for category, priority, and status.
changeStatusSteps: Open a ticket row or detail.
Pick status: Open → In Progress → Resolved when work is confirmed.
dashboard.resolutionBody: Under /admin/tickets, add resolution notes: what was done, parts, cost, time spent.
dashboard.overrideBody: If the bot misclassified a ticket, use Classification override to fix category, priority, or department. This is logged and feeds training data.
dashboard.exportBody: Export CSV exports what you currently see after filters.

calloutDoNot.title: Do not
list: Mark Resolved without confirmation from the technician.
Ignore URGENT tickets in the queue.

gaps.intro: Gaps are real user questions where the bot could not ground an answer in your FM documents. Closing a gap improves answers for everyone.
steps: Open the gap (or its detail page).
Write Content in English (the assistant runs in English).
Choose append (add to file) or overwrite (replace entire file).
Enable Auto-reindex so the bot picks up the text immediately.

calloutTip.title: Tip
calloutTip.body: Prefer append to existing topic files unless you are sure overwrite is safe.

calloutDoNot.title: Do not
list: Skip Auto-reindex and expect instant better answers.
Overwrite a large file without reading what is already there.

documents.intro: Markdown / text files in the FM corpus power retrieval. After any edit, run Reindex so embeddings match the files.
documents.uploadBody: Supported: .txt, .md, .csv, .pdf, .docx. Use Overwrite and Auto-reindex as needed.

calloutMistake.title: Most common mistake
calloutMistake.body: Editing a document and forgetting Reindex — users still get old answers until you reindex.

training.intro: Review examples produced from chat/tests. Approving good rows and fixing bad ones builds a high-quality dataset for future model work.
training.keyboardBody: Keyboard: ← → navigate, A approve, R reject.

calloutDoNot.title: Do not
calloutDoNot.body: Approve rows without checking category and priority. Bulk-reject pending without reading.

quality.intro: Mismatch groups show error patterns. Run eval runs a batch test (~80 cases). Analyze pending asks the analyzer model for suggested prompt additions (cached, rate-limited). Active overrides are live extra rules appended to the assistant instructions.
workflowSteps: Run eval to capture a baseline score.
Review mismatch groups so you know what is failing.
Run Analyze pending; read each suggestion carefully.
Apply only changes you understand; edit text in the modal if needed.
Run eval again later and compare accuracy; rollback if metrics worsen.

rows: Only one eval at a time
Wait for running to finish before starting another
Rollback old rules before adding new ones if you hit the cap
Fresh eval may be required before apply
Run eval if the UI says baseline is too old

calloutDoNot.title: Do not
list: Apply overrides you do not understand.
Ignore accuracy deltas after a change.
Run heavy eval repeatedly during peak chat hours if your API quota is tight.
